arts.ttl: Real Madrid vs Alaves, 2024 La Liga: Predicted lineups
arts.sum: If Ancelotti decides to give Mbappe the opportunity to rest, Endrick will feature in the starting lineup. Real Madrid predicted XI: Courtois, Vazquez, Tchouameni, Rudiger, Mendy, Valverde, Modric, Bellingham, Rodrygo, Vinicius, Endrick.

arts.ttl: ‘What a shame that he went to Real Madrid’ – Luis Enrique reflects on Kylian Mbappé’s PSG departure
arts.sum: Whilst Kylian Mbappé’s (25) final season at Paris Saint-Germain was a turbulent one, he is at least still remembered fondly by his former manager Luis Enrique. As Luis Enrique arrived…

arts.ttl: Rodrygo gets a goal in his 150th Real Madrid win: "Everything came off in the second half and we won the game"
arts.sum: Rodrygo struck Real Madrid's second goal against Espanyol on the way to his 150th victory as a madridista. In the pre-match team talk, the gaffer had told us we'd been poor in the first half of games and he wanted us to improve tonight.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid come from behind with a flurry of goals
arts.sum: They were forced to come from behind to take another three points, following Courtois' unfortunate 54th-minute own goal, but Ancelotti's boys produced a near-flawless 40 minutes, with goals for Carvajal, Rodrygo, Vini Jr. and Mbappé, who has now found the net in all four victories. One minute on, Modrić laid an inch-perfect ball in behind the defence, but the forward's left-footed effort was met by the inspired visiting goalkeeper once more.

arts.ttl: Ancelotti is set to coach his 300th Real Madrid game in the Alavés match
arts.sum: The Italian coach has so far led the team in 196 LaLiga games, 64 Champions League games, 24 Copa del Rey games, eight in the Spanish Super Cup, four in the Club World Cup and three in the European Super Cup . Both have won 14 trophies, in the case of Ancelotti 3 Champions League, 2 Club World Cups, 3 European Super Cups, 2 La Liga, 2 Copa del Rey and 2 Spanish Super Cups .

arts.ttl: How many games, trophies has Ancelotti won as Real Madrid coach?
arts.sum: Real Madrid’s clash with Alavés today will be a landmark occasion for Carlo Ancelotti, who takes charge of his 300th game as Los Blancos’ head coach . Ancelotti’s 299 games as Madrid boss break down as follows: 196 in LaLiga, 64 in the Champions League, 24 in the Copa del Rey, eight in the Spanish Super Cup, four in the Club World Cup and three in the UEFA Super Cup .

arts.ttl: Real Madrid rival Liverpool for transfer of PL star after Man United beat them to recent signing
arts.sum: Real Madrid are reportedly showing a strong interest in the potential transfer of Nottingham Forest centre-back Murillo, who is also liked by Liverpool and Chelsea. The talented Brazilian has impressed hugely during his time at Forest, and it’s little surprise to see that a host of top clubs are now potentially set to battle it […] The post Real Madrid rival Liverpool for transfer of PL star after Man United beat them to recent signing appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Arsenal & Chelsea battle Real Madrid for potential €45m wonderkid transfer
arts.sum: Arsenal and Chelsea are among the latest clubs to show a strong interest in the potential transfer of River Plate wonderkid Franco Mastantuono. The 17-year-old attacking midfielder is regarded as one of the most exciting young talents in South America at the moment, with a host of top European clubs scouting him, CaughtOffside understands. arts.ttl: Tuesday's La Liga predictions including Real Madrid vs. Alaves
arts.sum: Sports Mole provides score predictions and previews for all of today's La Liga fixtures, including Real Madrid vs. Alaves.

arts.ttl: Possible Real Madrid line-up for Alavés clash with Ancelotti set to rotate
arts.sum: Lucas Vázquez will replace Carvajal with the Spanish international picking up a slight knock in the game against Espanyol and Tchouameni could partner Rüdiger giving some respite to Militao with Mendy expected to return to left-back at the expense of Fran García. The potential XI for the Alavés clash could see: Courtois; Lucas Vázquez, Tchouameni, Rüdiger, Mendy; Modric, Valverde, Bellingham; Rodrygo, Endrick/Mbappé and Vinicius .
